title: podman still read the default path auth even if I specify `--authfile`
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug


**Description**
When using `--authfile` to specify a special auth file, podman still read the default path auths.

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Prepare an auth file that doesn't contain the `quay.io` registry auth, like below,
```console
[cloud-user@preserve-olm-env2 jian]$ cat config.json 
{
	"auths": {
		"localhost:5000": {
			"auth": "xxxx"
		}
	}
}
```
2. Pull a quay.io image by specifying the above auth file.
```console
}[cloud-user@preserve-olm-env2 jian]$podman pull --authfile config.json quay.io/openshift-qe-optional-operators/aosqe-index:v4.12
Trying to pull quay.io/openshift-qe-optional-operators/aosqe-index:v4.12...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 13c8e9a09860 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 072229712436 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 70fb9965a23f skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 0d1baa9a1e65 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 970e121bff39 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 0344f6f1fdad skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 1e94b7d87491 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob bb9a2c7b3a94 done  
Copying config 158a929aca done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
158a929acab1e8ffc1a5e710d84b25cbc6d275691b47c0d7b8cb0b637478b824
```

**Describe the results you received:**
That quay.io image can be pulled successfully without the auth!!!

**Describe the results you expected:**
That quay.io image should NOT be pulled successfully since there is no auth in the ` --authfile config.json` file.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
I guess Podman still read the default path config auth even if I specify the ` --authfile` file. So, it can pull that quay.io image successfully.

```console
[cloud-user@preserve-olm-env2 jian]$ cat /run/user/1000/containers/auth.json 
{
	"auths": {
		"quay.io": {
			"auth": "xxxx"
		}
	}
}
```

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
[cloud-user@preserve-olm-env2 jian]$ podman version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.1.1
API Version:  4.1.1
Go Version:   go1.17.7
Built:        Mon Jul 11 22:56:53 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman` or `brew info podman`):**

```
[cloud-user@preserve-olm-env2 jian]$ rpm -q podman
podman-4.1.1-2.module+el8.6.0+15917+093ca6f8.x86_64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked [the Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)?**


No

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
```console
[cloud-user@preserve-olm-env2 jian]$ cat /etc/redhat-release 
Red Hat Enterprise Linux release 8.4 (Ootpa)
[cloud-user@preserve-olm-env2 jian]$ uname -a
Linux preserve-olm-env2 4.18.0-287.el8.dt4.x86_64 #1 SMP Thu Feb 18 13:31:55 EST 2021 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out!

Good news: https://github.com/containers/image/commit/b3748aab5c90 has fixed that and will be shipped with the next version of Podman coming soon.
